Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Or Pending Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor Or Person With A Disability - Joseph
Off Calendar Sep-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action for the Minor, Joseph Silva.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq. appeared for petitioner Leslie Silva, Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Joseph Silva, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
Ms. Silva, appearing via CourtCall, was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Ms. Silva Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed on May 27, 2016.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $7,500.00.
Attorney Fees - $1,113.04.
Costs - $3,047.78.

The net balance of $3,339.18 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Mountain America Credit Union, Certerville Utah Branch, located at 298 N. Marketplace Drive, Centerville, UT 84014. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Furthermore, petitioner Leslie Silva is authorized and directed to deposit additional funds as they become available, without limitation and the the financial institution shall accept such deposits without need for additional orders of the Court.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action for the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to October 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the Status of the deposit of money into a Blocked Account.

(Mini Minutes, continued from previous entry)
No later than November 3, 2016 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, Advancing The Current Trial Date; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Oct-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Leslie Silva, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Miguel Silva, deceased, et al.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Shelley K. Tinkoff of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC
Alyssa Cohan of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants CBS Corporation and Thomas Dee Engineering Company
D. Scott Shaffer of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7000), appearing for defendant Honeywell International, Inc.
Taylor Day of MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS (415-442-1752), specially appearing by CourtCall for defendant Santa Fe Braun

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference and to advance trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(b). The July 10, 2017 trial date is advanced to February 14, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 17, 2017. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 2, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 30, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 13, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 6, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on February 9, 2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 27, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 10, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 2, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)
